subject: [RFC PATCH 15/45] mm: page_alloc: add per-superpageblock free lists

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch adds per-superpageblock free lists to the Linux kernel's page allocation system, enabling precise allocation steering by selecting the right superpageblock first and then allocating from its local free list in O(1). The patch introduces a new data structure for tracking free pages within each superpageblock, allowing for more efficient allocation of large memory blocks. It also updates various functions to use the per-superpageblock free lists, including __rmqueue_smallest, __rmqueue_claim, and __rmqueue_steal. This change aims to improve memory allocation performance by reducing the number of searches required to find available memory blocks.
